title: NIS2 Business Continuity and ICT Continuity Evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_bcp_test_register.csv
| eval test_date_epoch=strptime(last_tabletop_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval months_since=round((now()-test_date_epoch)/2592000,1)
| where months_since > 12
| join service_name [
    search index=itsi_summary earliest=-90d
    | where severity IN ("Critical","High")
    | stats sum(alert_duration) as downtime_seconds by service_name
]
| eval rto_seconds=rto_minutes*60
| eval rto_breach=if(downtime_seconds > rto_seconds, 1, 0)
| table service_name, months_since, downtime_seconds, rto_seconds, rto_breach
value: Business continuity, crisis management, and ICT continuity are explicit Article 21 themes. This use case monitors declared RTO/RPO adherence during real incidents and exercises, combining ITSI service degradation windows with BCP test completion records.
implementation: (1) Record BCP/ICT continuity test dates per critical service; (2) map services to ITSI entities; (3) alert when annual tabletop is overdue; (4) measure downtime during sev1 incidents against RTO; (5) integrate with UC-22.2.17 for backup evidence.
visualization: Table (overdue tests and RTO breaches), Timeline (tests and incidents), Single value (services breaching RTO).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
